### **2. tabs-demo.tsx**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393184095-27gf41/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/tabs-demo.tsx`

- **Export:** Named default export as `TabsDemo()` function

### **3. accordion-demo.tsx**
**File:** `/private/var/folders/vq/9q38rw0x2455zr_whqcrjbhr0000gn/T/planmode-bench-wt-run-1775393184095-27gf41/apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/accordion-demo.tsx`

**Key Characteristics:**
- **Imports:** Only UI components (Accordion, AccordionContent, AccordionItem, AccordionTrigger) - no external libraries or icons
- **Export:** Named default export as `AccordionDemo()` function
- **"use client":** Not included

- **JSX Structure:** Accordion wrapper with multiple AccordionItem elements; each item has AccordionTrigger (header) and AccordionContent (body with flex column and gap)
- **Interactivity:** Click to expand/collapse (built into Accordion component)
- **Styling:** Consistent use of utility classes (`w-full`, `flex`, `flex-col`, `gap-4`, `text-balance`)

## Analysis of shadcn/ui v4 Registry Pattern

Based on my thorough analysis of the registry files, here's a complete breakdown:

### TypeScript Type Structure

The registry uses types defined in `/packages/shadcn/src/registry/schema.ts`. The key types are:

**Registry Item Type:**
```typescript
type RegistryItem = z.infer<typeof registryItemSchema>
```

**Common Fields (all items share these):**
- `name: string` - Unique identifier
- `type: "registry:ui" | "registry:example" | "registry:hook" | "registry:lib" | ... ` (see enum below)
- `dependencies?: string[]` - NPM package dependencies
- `devDependencies?: string[]` - Dev dependencies
- `registryDependencies?: string[]` - Dependencies on other registry items
- `files: RegistryItemFile[]` - Array of files to install
- `title?: string` - Display title
- `description?: string` - Description text
- `docs?: string` - Optional markdown documentation
- `categories?: string[]` - Category tags
- `author?: string` - Author name
- `tailwind?: { config?: {...} }` - Tailwind config overrides
- `cssVars?: { light?: {...}, dark?: {...} }` - CSS custom properties
- `meta?: Record<string, any>` - Custom metadata

**Type Enum Values:**
```
"registry:lib", "registry:block", "registry:component", "registry:ui", 
"registry:hook", "registry:page", "registry:file", "registry:theme", 
"registry:style", "registry:item", "registry:base", "registry:font",
"registry:example" (internal), "registry:internal"
```

**File Entry Structure:**
```typescript
{
  path: string;              // Path to file (relative format)
  type: "registry:ui" | "registry:hook" | "registry:example" | ...;
  content?: string;          // Optional inline content
  target?: string;           // For registry:file/registry:page
}
```

---

### UI Components Registry (`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/ui/_registry.ts`)

**File Path Format:** `"ui/component-name.tsx"` - Relative paths within the registry

**Entry Pattern - Basic (no dependencies):**
```typescript
{
  name: "card",
  type: "registry:ui",
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/card.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Entry Pattern - With NPM dependencies:**
```typescript
{
  name: "accordion",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/accordion.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Entry Pattern - With registryDependencies:**
```typescript
{
  name: "alert-dialog",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],
  registryDependencies: ["button"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/alert-dialog.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Entry Pattern - Multiple Dependencies:**
```typescript
{
  name: "calendar",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["react-day-picker@latest", "date-fns"],
  registryDependencies: ["button"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/calendar.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Entry Pattern - Multiple Files (mixed types):**
```typescript
{
  name: "toast",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/toast.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
    {
      path: "hooks/use-toast.ts",
      type: "registry:hook",
    },
    {
      path: "ui/toaster.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Entry Pattern - With Tailwind Config and CSS Variables:**
```typescript
{
  name: "sidebar",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui", "class-variance-authority", "lucide-react"],
  registryDependencies: [
    "button",
    "separator",
    "sheet",
    "tooltip",
    "input",
    "use-mobile",
    "skeleton",
  ],
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/sidebar.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
  tailwind: {
    config: {
      theme: {
        extend: {
          colors: {
            sidebar: {
              DEFAULT: "hsl(var(--sidebar-background))",
              foreground: "hsl(var(--sidebar-foreground))",
              // ... more colors
            },
          },
        },
      },
    },
  },
  cssVars: {
    light: {
      "sidebar-background": "0 0% 98%",
      "sidebar-foreground": "240 5.3% 26.1%",
      // ... more vars
    },
    dark: {
      "sidebar-background": "240 5.9% 10%",
      "sidebar-foreground": "240 4.8% 95.9%",
      // ... more vars
    },
  },
}
```

**Entry Pattern - With Documentation:**
```typescript
{
  name: "tooltip",
  type: "registry:ui",
  dependencies: ["radix-ui"],
  docs: `The \`tooltip\` component has been added. Remember to wrap your app with the \`TooltipProvider\` component.

\`\`\`tsx title="app/layout.tsx"
import { TooltipProvider } from "@/components/ui/tooltip"

export default function RootLayout({ children }: { children: React.ReactNode }) {
  return (
    <html lang="en">
      <body>
        <TooltipProvider>{children}</TooltipProvider>
      </body>
    </html>
  )
}
\`\`\`
`,
  files: [
    {
      path: "ui/tooltip.tsx",
      type: "registry:ui",
    },
  ],
}
```

---

### Examples Registry (`apps/v4/registry/new-york-v4/examples/_registry.ts`)

**File Path Format:** `"examples/example-name.tsx"` - Relative paths within the registry

**Entry Pattern - Simple Example (single file):**
```typescript
{
  name: "accordion-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["accordion"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/accordion-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Entry Pattern - Example with Multiple Registry Dependencies:**
```typescript
{
  name: "form-next-complex",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: [
    "field",
    "input",
    "textarea",
    "button",
    "card",
    "spinner",
    "checkbox",
    "dialog",
    "radio-group",
    "select",
    "switch",
  ],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/form-next-complex.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
}
```

**Entry Pattern - Example with NPM Dependencies:**
```typescript
{
  name: "form-rhf-demo",
  type: "registry:example",
  registryDependencies: ["field", "input", "input-group", "button", "card"],
  files: [
    {
      path: "examples/form-rhf-demo.tsx",
      type: "registry:example",
    },
  ],
  dependencies: ["react-hook-form", "@hookform/resolvers", "zod"],
}
```

---

### Directory Structures

**UI Components Directory Files (55 components):**
```
accordion.tsx, alert-dialog.tsx, alert.tsx, aspect-ratio.tsx, avatar.tsx,
badge.tsx, breadcrumb.tsx, button-group.tsx, button.tsx, calendar.tsx,
card.tsx, carousel.tsx, chart.tsx, checkbox.tsx, collapsible.tsx,
combobox.tsx, command.tsx, context-menu.tsx, dialog.tsx, direction.tsx,
drawer.tsx, dropdown-menu.tsx, empty.tsx, field.tsx, form.tsx,
hover-card.tsx, input-group.tsx, input-otp.tsx, input.tsx, item.tsx,
kbd.tsx, label.tsx, menubar.tsx, native-select.tsx, navigation-menu.tsx,
pagination.tsx, popover.tsx, progress.tsx, radio-group.tsx, resizable.tsx,
scroll-area.tsx, select.tsx, separator.tsx, sheet.tsx, sidebar.tsx,
skeleton.tsx, slider.tsx, sonner.tsx, spinner.tsx, switch.tsx, table.tsx,
tabs.tsx, textarea.tsx, toggle-group.tsx, toggle.tsx, tooltip.tsx
```

**Examples Directory (235+ examples) - Naming Convention:**
- `{component-name}-demo.tsx` - Basic demo example
- `{component-name}-{variant}.tsx` - Variant examples (e.g., `button-destructive.tsx`, `badge-outline.tsx`)
- `{component-name}-{feature}.tsx` - Feature examples (e.g., `button-with-icon.tsx`, `input-with-label.tsx`)
- Form examples use multiple patterns:
  - `form-{framework}-demo.tsx` (e.g., `form-rhf-demo.tsx`, `form-tanstack-demo.tsx`)
  - `form-{framework}-{field-type}.tsx` (e.g., `form-rhf-input.tsx`)
  - `form-next-demo.tsx`, `form-next-complex.tsx` (Server actions)

**Registry Directory Structure (new-york-v4):**
```
new-york-v4/
├── ui/              (55 component files + _registry.ts)
├── examples/        (235+ example files + _registry.ts)
├── blocks/          (block components registry)
├── internal/        (internal utilities registry)
├── charts/          (chart component registry)
├── hooks/           (custom hooks registry)
└── lib/             (utility libraries registry)
```

---

### Key Observations

1. **Path Format:** All paths are relative to their registry directory (e.g., `"ui/button.tsx"`, `"examples/button-demo.tsx"`, `"hooks/use-toast.ts"`)

2. **Type Consistency:** Both entry-level `type` and file-level `type` must match the context (UI entries have files with `type: "registry:ui"`, examples have `type: "registry:example"`, etc.)

3. **Dependency Resolution:**
   - `dependencies` = NPM packages (install via npm/yarn)
   - `registryDependencies` = other registry items (must be satisfied from the same registry)
   - Both are optional and can coexist

4. **Multiple Files Pattern:** Used for components that need multiple implementations (e.g., `toast` needs the component, hook, and provider)

5. **Schema Validation:** All items conform to the `RegistryItem` Zod schema with discriminated unions by `type`

6. **Optional Features:**
   - `tailwind.config` - Extend Tailwind configuration
   - `cssVars` - Define CSS custom properties with light/dark variants
   - `docs` - Markdown documentation (supports code blocks with syntax highlighting)
   - `meta` - Custom metadata for tooling

```

**Examples Directory (235+ examples) - Naming Convention:**
- `{component-name}-demo.tsx` - Basic demo example
- `{component-name}-{variant}.tsx` - Variant examples (e.g., `button-destructive.tsx`, `badge-outline.tsx`)
- `{component-name}-{feature}.tsx` - Feature examples (e.g., `button-with-icon.tsx`, `input-with-label.tsx`)
- Form examples use multiple patterns:
  - `form-{framework}-demo.tsx` (e.g., `form-rhf-demo.tsx`, `form-tanstack-demo.tsx`)
  - `form-{framework}-{field-type}.tsx` (e.g., `form-rhf-input.tsx`)
  - `form-next-demo.tsx`, `form-next-complex.tsx` (Server actions)

**Registry Directory Structure (new-york-v4):**
```
new-york-v4/
├── ui/              (55 component files + _registry.ts)
├── examples/        (235+ example files + _registry.ts)
├── blocks/          (block components registry)
├── internal/        (internal utilities registry)
├── charts/          (chart component registry)
├── hooks/           (custom hooks registry)
└── lib/             (utility libraries registry)
```

---

### Key Observations

1. **Path Format:** All paths are relative to their registry directory (e.g., `"ui/button.tsx"`, `"examples/button-demo.tsx"`, `"hooks/use-toast.ts"`)

2. **Type Consistency:** Both entry-level `type` and file-level `type` must match the context (UI entries have files with `type: "registry:ui"`, examples have `type: "registry:example"`, etc.)

3. **Dependency Resolution:**
   - `dependencies` = NPM packages (install via npm/yarn)
   - `registryDependencies` = other registry items (must be satisfied from the same registry)
   - Both are optional and can coexist

4. **Multiple Files Pattern:** Used for components that need multiple implementations (e.g., `toast` needs the component, hook, and provider)

5. **Schema Validation:** All items conform to the `RegistryItem` Zod schema with discriminated unions by `type`

6. **Optional Features:**
   - `tailwind.config` - Extend Tailwind configuration
   - `cssVars` - Define CSS custom properties with light/dark variants
   - `docs` - Markdown documentation (supports code blocks with syntax highlighting)
   - `meta` - Custom metadata for tooling
